title: Primavera Unifier Solution Architect
description:
About Us:
Consertus is a global capital program management and advisory firm that combines deep human expertise with advanced digital tools to deliver smarter, faster, and more sustainable outcomes. Headquartered in Miami, Florida, and supported by a team of more than 1,000 professionals worldwide, we help clients plan, execute, and optimize large-scale capital programs.
Our integrated services span digital, advisory, and delivery, empowering organizations to navigate complexity, manage risk, and achieve strategic goals in sectors including aviation, transportation, transit, water, healthcare, energy, education, government, commercial, life sciences, and technology.
At Consertus, we celebrate individual strengths, foster strong relationships, and promote flexibility in how and where we work. Join us to grow your career, develop your skills, and make a lasting impact as we transform how the world is built.
Discover how Consertus is driving transformative change for our clients, our people, and the communities we serve at 
www.consertus.com
  
About this Role: 
The Unifier Solution Architect designs and leads the delivery of Oracle Primavera Unifier solutions that replace manual, paper, and spreadsheet-based processes with a governed, scalable system of record. This role partners with business and project stakeholders to define requirements, translate them into Unifier configurations, and ensure solutions are usable, maintainable, and aligned to program controls and governance.
Key Responsibilities:
Lead the design of Unifier and other PMIS solutions 
Work directly with clients to gather requirements, make recommendations, take detailed notes, and convey those to other members of the project team 
Create strategy documents, such as integration strategy, PMIS strategy, training strategy, etc
Create detailed design documentation, and artifacts
Work with developers and development leads to manage Unifier/PMIS development
Be able to create proof of concepts to demonstrate key functionality
Quality assurance on solutions developed by digital teams
Execute UAT process and obtain final sign off from clients
Assist with the development of integration and migration plans 
Escalate any issues promptly to project leadership
Demo solutions to clients 
Ability to manage shifting priorities and adapt quickly 
Support sales process as needed
Required Skills and Experience: 
5+ Years Experience with Oracle Primavera Unifier or similar PMIS
3+ Years Solution Architecture experience with Unifier or similar PMIS
Excellent communication skills (virtual meetings, phone calls, emails, etc)
Proficient with Microsoft Office Suite
Nice to Have:
2-3 Years Construction experience
Experience with building Integrations
Power BI experience
Education:
Bachelor’s degree in engineering, Computer Science, or Information Technology (or equivalent).
Equal Employment Opportunity Statement:
Consertus is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and identities, and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, disability, age, veteran status, or any other protected status.

title: Senior Cost Manager/Program Controls Manager
description:
About Us:
Consertus is a global capital program management and advisory firm that combines deep human expertise with advanced digital tools to deliver smarter, faster, and more sustainable outcomes. Headquartered in Miami, Florida, and supported by a team of more than 1,000 professionals worldwide, we help clients plan, execute, and optimize large-scale capital programs.
Our integrated services span digital, advisory, and delivery, empowering organizations to navigate complexity, manage risk, and achieve strategic goals in sectors including aviation, transportation, transit, water, healthcare, energy, education, government, commercial, life sciences, and technology.
At Consertus, we celebrate individual strengths, foster strong relationships, and promote flexibility in how and where we work. Join us to grow your career, develop your skills, and make a lasting impact as we transform how the world is built.
Discover how Consertus is driving transformative change for our clients, our people, and the communities we serve at 
www.consertus.com
  
About this Role: 
Consertus is seeking a 
Senior Cost Manager/Senior Program Controls Manager
 
(Healthcare Capital Programs)
 based out of 
Seattle, WA.
 In this role, you will wear two hats, own end-to-end cost management while also providing senior-level program controls support to integrate cost, schedule, and risk into clear, decision-ready reporting. This individual contributor role is accountable for budget governance, forecast (EAC) accuracy, and executive-ready insights across multiple projects, partnering closely with project management, healthcare clients, and technical stakeholders to drive financial transparency, risk management, and cost performance.
The ideal candidate brings strong construction cost management experience, knowledge of healthcare environments, and the versatility to move between detailed analysis and program-level controls leadership. You are comfortable switching from building forecasts, validating pay applications, and managing change/trends to facilitating controls cadence, aligning stakeholders, and translating complex data into clear narratives and recommendations. Success in this role requires influencing outcomes without direct authority, proactively surfacing risks and opportunities, and driving timely decisions through stakeholder alignment.
Key Responsibilities
Lead cost management for a portfolio of healthcare-related construction and infrastructure projects (hospitals, clinics, campus utilities), establishing consistent cost coding/WBS alignment and reporting governance.
Own project/program budgets, committed costs, forecast (EAC), and cash flow from preconstruction through closeout; ensure forecast integrity through disciplined reviews and variance explanations.
Develop and maintain cost trend and risk registers; evaluate impacts to contingency, escalation, and exposure, and recommend mitigation actions and recovery plans.
Lead cost change control by evaluating scope/design/phasing changes, maintaining change logs, and supporting negotiation strategy with clear entitlement and backup documentation.
Reconcile contractor/consultant cost reports with Consertus and client systems; challenge assumptions and align stakeholders on a single source of truth.
Prepare executive-ready weekly and monthly cost reports and narratives, including key drivers, variances, risks/opportunities, decisions required, and forward-looking outlook.
Partner with Project Managers, Project Controls, Scheduling, and Accounting to integrate schedule progress, quantities, and earned progress into accurate cost-to-complete forecasting.
Review and validate pay applications, invoices, and cost accruals as applicable; support audit-ready documentation and compliance with client/owner requirements.
Track job cost performance against KPIs and healthcare-specific constraints (phasing, infection control, stakeholder coordination), escalating concerns early with recommended options.
Support project closeout, including validation of final costs, change reconciliation, lessons learned, and support for owner audits and financial turnover.
Interface with owner representatives, design teams, and contractors to lead cost review meetings, drive timely responses, and maintain transparency across stakeholders.
Continuously improve cost management processes, templates, and controls; coach project teams on best practices to improve consistency and auditability (without direct people management responsibility).
Qualifications
Requirements:
Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Construction Management, Finance, Accounting, or a related field
7+ years of progressive experience in construction cost management/cost engineering, preferably on healthcare, institutional, or large-scale infrastructure capital programs
Demonstrated ownership of budgets, committed cost, forecast (EAC), and cash flow, with the ability to explain variances and forecast drivers to executive and non-financial stakeholders
Strong working knowledge of construction accounting principles, cost forecasting, project controls, and cost governance (coding structures, controls, auditability)
Experience with change control, trend/risk management, contingency planning, and cost-to-complete development across design and construction phases
Proficiency with cost control and reporting tools; experience with cost management systems and dashboards required.
Ability to influence outcomes without direct authority; strong facilitation skills for cost review meetings and stakeholder alignment
High attention to detail, organization, and follow-through in a fast-paced, multi-project environment
Familiarity with the unique challenges of working on active healthcare campuses (phasing, infection control, stakeholder coordination) is a plus
Preferred: CCP (AACE), CCM (CMAA), PMP, or equivalent certification
Senior Program Controls Manager – Additional Requirements (Preferred):
7+ years of progressive experience in project/program controls on large capital programs; supporting multi-project portfolios or program management offices (PMO)
Demonstrated expertise integrating cost, schedule, and risk into a cohesive controls framework (baseline development, updates, variance analysis, and corrective-action planning)
Advanced proficiency with scheduling and controls tools (Primavera P6 preferred) and program controls platforms/reporting (e.g., Unifier, EcoSys, SAP/Oracle, Power BI), including data governance and auditability
Experience establishing and enforcing program controls standards: progress measurement rules, change control workflows, monthly reporting cadence, and KPI definitions
Strong capability in schedule health assessment, critical path evaluation, and schedule-driven forecasting; ability to connect progress/production signals to cost-to-complete outcomes
Executive-level communication skills, including preparation and delivery of steering committee materials (drivers, trends, risks, decisions required) and facilitation of cross-functional reviews
Preferred certifications: PMI-SP, PSP (AACE), CCP (AACE), PMP, CCM, or equivalent
Working Hours:
 On-Site, Full-Time, Exempt
Compensation Range:
 $180,000-$250,000 annually, depending on skills, experience and education.
What’s In It For You
Comprehensive health coverage (medical, dental, and vision)
Company-paid life and disability insurance
401(k) with day-one eligibility. We contribute dollar for dollar up to the first 3% of your contributions. Then we match 50% for the next 2% of your contributions.
Generous time off: 10 paid holidays and PTO starting at 15 days, growing up to 25
Access to Consertus Academy for continuous learning and development
How to Apply: 
If you’re passionate about this role we’d love to hear from you. Apply today!
Equal Employment Opportunity Statement:
Consertus is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and identities, and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, disability, age, veteran status, or any other protected status.

title: Claims and Change Specialist
location: New York, New York, United States
description:
About the Position

We’re seeking a 
Claims and Change Specialist
 to support large, heavy-civil infrastructure programs, including highways, roads, bridges, transit (both rail and bus), international airports, and water/wastewater projects delivered through both traditional and alternative methods. Your primary focus will be supporting claims analysis, contract administration, and change management activities associated with complex infrastructure construction projects, including contract research, change control, commercial management support, and coordination with project controls and construction management teams.

This is a long-term, full-time, on-site position located in New York, NY.

Responsibilities
 
may include, but are not limited to, the following:

Support the review of contractor notices, change requests, contract modifications, and related documentation through contract research and claims analysis.

Assist in evaluating entitlement considerations by reviewing contract requirements, technical documentation, project records, and applicable procedures.

Draft correspondence related to contract changes, requests for clarification, and change management activities for management review.

Prepare written summaries, analyses, and supporting documentation outlining contractual considerations, findings, and key project issues.

Support administration of the change control process in accordance with contract requirements, project procedures, and established change management practices.

Review and track change requests to help ensure compliance with contractual requirements, documentation standards, and established timelines.

Compile, maintain, and organize supporting documentation associated with change requests, including cost, schedule, technical, and project controls information.

Maintain accurate records of change orders, approvals, correspondence, negotiations, and supporting documentation within project tracking systems and change logs.

Coordinate with estimators, schedulers, project controls teams, construction management staff, contractors, and stakeholders to facilitate the collection and organization of information related to proposed changes.

Schedule and coordinate change management meetings, prepare meeting materials, and document action items, decisions, and follow-up activities.

Assist in the preparation and review of change proposal requests and related documentation.

Support change order negotiations by coordinating meetings, preparing materials, documenting discussions, and tracking outcomes.

Assist in ensuring change management activities comply with applicable contract requirements, procurement guidelines, project procedures, and audit requirements.

Organize and maintain project records to support internal and external audits related to contract compliance and change management activities.

Attributes

Excellent written and verbal communication and interpersonal skills.

Excellent organizational and analytical skills.

Collaborative mindset that fosters teamwork, trust, and positive relationships.

Natural curiosity, problem-solving abilities, and a passion for continuous improvement.

Ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines in a fast-paced project environment.

Strong attention to detail and commitment to quality, accountability, and professionalism.

Minimum Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in engineering, construction management, business, or related field, or equivalent combination of education and experience.

2-5 years of experience supporting claims analysis, contract administration, change management, project controls, commercial management, or construction management activities on large infrastructure, transportation, transit, rail, aviation, or heavy civil construction projects.

Experience reviewing and interpreting construction contracts, specifications, change orders, contract modifications, notices, and other project documentation.

Experience supporting change management, contract administration, project controls, commercial management, or claims-related activities on design-build and/or alternative delivery projects.

Experience coordinating with estimators, schedulers, project controls teams, construction management teams, contractors, and project stakeholders.

Demonstrated experience in technical writing and project documentation.

Experience maintaining detailed project records, logs, correspondence, and supporting documentation.

Experience using Oracle Primavera Unifier or similar project controls, contract management, or change management software (Unifier preferred).

Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite/Office 365 (e.g., Outlook, Teams, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, etc.).

Preferred Qualifications

Master’s degree in construction management, engineering, or business.

Experience supporting claims analysis, contract administration, change management, or commercial management on major transit, rail, tunnel, underground, or heavy civil infrastructure programs.

Experience supporting owner's representative, PMCM, program management, or construction management assignments.

Experience supporting design-build contracts exceeding $100M in construction value.

Experience supporting contract change negotiations and commercial management activities.

NYC agency, public transportation, or major capital infrastructure program experience.

Compensation Details

Expected Salary: $115k-$145k/year ($55-$70/hour). title: Document Control Lead
description:
About the Position
 

We’re seeking a 
Document Control Lead 
t
o support a large-scale aviation redevelopment program delivered within a complex public-sector infrastructure environment. This role focuses on maintaining accurate, timely, and compliant project documentation while also supporting the development, implementation, and standardization of program-level document control procedures, protocols, governance materials, etc. 
 

This is a long-term, full-time, hybrid positi
on (4 days per week onsite subject to change based on project requirements) location in Queens, NY.
 

Responsibilities 
may include, but are not limited to, the following:
 

Support implementation and maintenance of program-wide document control protocols and standards.
 

Maintain accurate document naming conventions, metadata tagging, version control, and coding structures in alignment with project and program requirements.

Track deliverables and support coordination of document reviews between internal teams, consultants, contractors, and external stakeholders.

Support development and maintenance of document control governance materials including procedures, protocols, management plans, and Work Instruction Documents.

.   
 

Attributes
 

Excellent written communication skills with the ability to prepare clear, structured, and consistent governance documentation.
 

Strong attention to detail, organization, and document accuracy.
 

Ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines in fast-paced, large-scale program environments.

Bachelor’s degree in engineering, construction management, business administration, project management, or related field, or equivalent combination of education and experience.

Experience managing document versioning, metadata tagging, naming conventions, and controlled document workflows.
 

Experience supporting document control processes for RFIs, submittals, drawings, specifications, meeting minutes, and project correspondence.
 

Demonstrated experience preparing governance documentation such as procedures, protocols, management plans, guidance documents, or Work Instruction Documents.
 

Experience supporting enterprise document management systems such as SharePoint, eBuilder, Aconex, ProjectWise, Procore, Unifier, or similar platforms. 
 

Strong Microsoft Office Suite/Office 365 skills (e.g., Outlook, Teams, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, etc.)
.

Experience supporting aviation, transportation, transit, or public agency capital improvement programs.
 

Experience supporting multi-project or program-level document control environments.

title: Document Control Manager
description:
About the Position

We’re seeking a 
Document Control Manager
 to support large, complex infrastructure programs, including highways, roads, bridges, transit (rail and bus), international airports, and water/wastewater projects delivered through both traditional and alternative delivery methods. Your primary focus will be leading document control operations, information governance, and enterprise document management initiatives that support program delivery, regulatory compliance, and organizational performance across major capital programs. This role will oversee document control personnel, establish and maintain program-wide standards, and support implementation and optimization of Electronic Document Management Systems (EDMS). The position requires close collaboration with project teams, consultants, contractors, quality management personnel, information technology staff, and executive leadership.

This is a long-term, full-time, on-site position located in Sacramento, CA.

Responsibilities
 
may include, but are not limited to, the following:

Lead, manage, and continuously improve document control operations supporting large, complex infrastructure programs.

Supervise, mentor, and develop document control staff while establishing workload priorities and performance expectations.

Develop, implement, and maintain document control policies, procedures, standards, templates, and governance practices.

Oversee implementation, administration, optimization, and adoption of Electronic Document Management Systems (EDMS) and related document management technologies.

Coordinate document control activities among project teams, consultants, contractors, and stakeholders to ensure consistency and compliance.

Establish and monitor key performance indicators, dashboards, reporting tools, and program metrics to support executive decision-making.

Support business process improvement initiatives, workflow automation efforts, and operational efficiency enhancements.

Collaborate with information technology, records management, quality management, and project stakeholders to implement document management solutions.

Monitor compliance with contractual requirements, document control procedures, regulatory requirements, and quality standards.

Support audits, quality reviews, corrective action programs, litigation support efforts, public records requests, and information governance initiatives.

Prepare executive-level reports, presentations, recommendations, and program performance assessments.

Support project closeout activities, records retention requirements, and document turnover processes.

Attributes

Excellent written and verbal communication and interpersonal skills.

Strong leadership, organizational, and team development capabilities.

Collaborative mindset that fosters trust, accountability, and stakeholder alignment.

Strategic problem-solving abilities with a focus on process improvement and operational excellence.

Strong attention to detail and commitment to quality, compliance, and continuous improvement.

Minimum Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in business administration, information management, construction management, or related field, or equivalent combination of education and experience.

10+ years of document control experience supporting large, complex construction projects, infrastructure programs, or capital improvement initiatives, including 2 years of demonstrated experience leading and managing a document controls team.

Experience supporting implementation, configuration, administration, or optimization of Electronic Document Management Systems (EDMS).

Experience coordinating document control requirements among owners, consultants, contractors, project teams, and executive stakeholders.

Knowledge of information governance, records management, document retention requirements, and document lifecycle management.

Experience supporting audits, compliance reviews, quality management initiatives, and corrective action programs.

Ability to analyze operational performance, identify improvement opportunities, and implement effective solutions.

Experience developing performance metrics, dashboards, reporting tools, and executive-level presentations.

Experience with workflow automation tools and business process improvement initiatives.

Skilled in automated workflow tools such as Power Automate, Power BI, or similar platforms, and experienced in technical training delivery.

Proficiency with document management platforms, EDMS technologies, and enterprise collaboration systems.

Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite/Office 365 (e.g., Outlook, Teams, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, SharePoint, etc.).

Preferred Qualifications

title: Project Scheduler
description:
About the Position

We’re seeking a 
Project Scheduler
 to support a portfolio of major transit capital improvement projects. Your primary focus will be developing, maintaining, and analyzing project schedules for the owner's Project Controls team, supporting projects from planning and design through construction and closeout while helping ensure successful project delivery.

This is a long-term, full-time, on-site position located in San Jose, CA.

Responsibilities
 
may include, but are not limited to, the following:

Develop, maintain, and update master schedules, detailed project schedules, schedule forecasts, recovery schedules, and schedule reports for a portfolio of transportation capital projects.

Manage and regularly update schedules for approximately 80 to 100 active projects throughout planning, design, procurement, and construction.

Analyze project schedules to identify critical path activities, schedule risks, constraints, and opportunities for schedule improvement.

Review contractor baseline schedules, monthly schedule updates, recovery schedules, and schedule narratives for compliance with contract requirements.

Perform Time Impact Analysis (TIA) to evaluate schedule impacts associated with contract changes, delays, and change orders.

Coordinate closely with project managers, project controls staff, consultants, contractors, and stakeholders to maintain accurate project schedules and resolve schedule-related issues.

Integrate individual project schedules into larger program or master schedules to support program-level reporting and decision-making.

Support project controls activities involving schedule performance, cost controls, funding, earned value concepts, and project reporting.

Prepare schedule analyses, progress reports, executive dashboards, and other management reports that communicate project status, schedule performance, and forecasted outcomes.

Provide scheduling requirements and schedule specifications for inclusion in procurement and construction contract documents.

Evaluate contractor work sequencing, construction methods, productivity assumptions, and schedule logic to verify schedule reasonableness.

Support contract administration activities involving schedule analysis, change management, claims avoidance, and schedule documentation.

Coordinate with internal departments and external agencies to support project planning, delivery, and reporting requirements.

Attributes

Excellent written and verbal communication and interpersonal skills.

Excellent organizational and analytical skills.

Collaborative mindset that fosters teamwork, trust, and positive relationships.

Natural curiosity, problem-solving abilities, and a passion for continuous improvement.

Ability to communicate schedule status, risks, and recommendations to both technical and executive stakeholders.

Strong attention to detail and commitment to quality, accountability, and professionalism.

Minimum Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in engineering, construction management, architecture, or equivalent combination of education and experience.

5+ years of experience developing, maintaining, and analyzing schedules for transit or other similar heavy civil infrastructure capital projects.

Strong knowledge of Critical Path Method (CPM) scheduling principles, schedule development, schedule analysis, and schedule forecasting.

Experience developing and reviewing contractor baseline schedules and monthly schedule updates.

Experience performing Time Impact Analysis (TIA) and evaluating schedule impacts associated with contract changes and delays.

Knowledge of earned value concepts and their application to project controls and schedule performance measurement.

Understanding of construction sequencing, construction means and methods, contract administration, and change management.

Experience coordinating with project managers, contractors, consultants, and owner representatives throughout project delivery.

Experience preparing schedule analyses, progress reports, executive reporting, and schedule performance metrics.

Proficiency with Oracle Primavera P6, Oracle Primavera Unifier, Microsoft Project, and other project controls software.

Proficiency with Microsoft Office 365 (e.g., Outlook, Teams, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, etc.).

Preferred Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in engineering, construction management, or architecture.

Planning & Scheduling Professional (PSP), Project Management Professional (PMP), Certified Cost Professional (CCP), or equivalent certification.

Experience supporting owner’s representative, program management, or construction management assignments.

Experience supporting design-build, CM/GC, or progressive design-build delivery methods.

Experience evaluating construction claims and schedule entitlement.

Our Deloitte Strategy & Transactions team helps guide clients through their most critical moments and transformational initiatives. Do you want to help clients identify key business issues to drive meaningful change? Collaborating with project teams to ensure the successful rollout of systems such as Oracle Primavera Unifier, Kahua, EcoSys, and Procore

2+ years of experience in project management and project controls
Experience with project controls activities, including planning, budgeting, and forecasting, and integrating those activities into technology platforms
Experience configuring PMIS tools, performing system integrations, and developing reports and dashboard
Experience using Microsoft Excel, Microsoft PowerPoint, and Microsoft Visio

In response to this market trend, the Renewables & Clean Power (R&CP) team have signed an early works contract to build one of the largest gas fired power generation facilities in the U.S.

The candidate may start in the home office in Reston, Houston or Cheyenne, Wyoming, as single status, to support preconstruction planning, ahead of project mobilization to the field when the candidate will locate on a long-term assignment to Cheyenne, Wyoming. Job Summary:
 
In this role, you will lead, implement, and maintain global construction field welding programs and provide specialized welding engineering support. You will manage, inspect, and monitor the welding test lab, the rod room, the weld heat treatment program, and the laboratory quality plan. Your technical advice will help ensure defects, behaviors, and other issues are rectified prior to final inspection. 
#LI-BH1
Major Responsibilities:
 
Coordinates work activities for Project Field Engineer and the Lead Field Welding Engineer in implementing the site welding program on medium sized projects.
Implements and maintains Bechtel Field Welding program or all welding operations.
Provides specialized welding engineering support for construction to ensure construction meets all code, client/engineering specifications and Bechtel procedures and standards.
Assists in the management of the pre- and post-weld heat treatment program.
Assists in the management of the rod room and rod room attendant to ensure all weld filler materials are controlled so to meet Bechtel, Code, and Client requirements.
Schedules and coordinates NDE with construction and subcontractors for the most efficient NDE schedule at the least cost and least impact to construction.
Works with the team to review and keep up to date with weld documents and ensures the credibility/accountability of the Welding Program.
Performs field inspections of welding work, and advises on rectification of quality behaviors and defects prior to final inspection
May be responsible for the implementation and leads the subcontractors and direct hire Pre/PWHT program.
Education and Experience Requirements:
 
Requires bachelor's degree (or international equivalent) and 8-10 years of relevant experience or 12-14 years of relevant work experience​
Required Knowledge and Skills:
 
NDE Certs including VT, PT and MT
Knowledge of AWS, API, NFPA, AWWA, ASTM and ASNT codes, specifications, and standards.
Heavy industrial direct hire construction experience commensurate with the type of work to be undertaken.
Advanced knowledge and/or certifications in SMAW / GTAW / GMAW / FCAW.
Intermediate understanding of Preheat/Post weld heat treatment of equipment and consumables.
Knowledge of engineering and construction methods and processes customarily acquired over time through specialized instruction or practical experience.
Demonstrated ability to problem-solve in high pressure situations.
Strong technical background and knowledge of all phases of welding applications, and non-destructive examination relating to construction activities.
Understanding of applicable Bechtel work processes including Standard Work Process Procedures.
Advanced knowledge of Piping Design Drawings/Specifications and Teamwork’s/WITS.
Must be certifiable by written examination to Bechtel's nondestructive examination requirements as NDE Level II Visual Examination.
Knowledge and understanding of Bechtel's SWPP 4MP-T81-03701 Welding Program, 03708 Pre/Post Weld Heat Treatment and 03705 Welding & NDE Documentation.
Familiarity with Bechtel Standard Applications (BSAPs) including Unifier, Aconex, DSCS, PTR, Agile, Navisworks, WITs, Teamwork’s, Arc Stryker, Pipe Fighter, and other automation tools which are used on Bechtel projects.

The project is a 1.3 GW simple-cycle power generation facility located in Millersport, Ohio, being constructed adjacent to a hyperscale data center campus. The facility includes multiple power generation technologies consisting of Solar Titan 130 gas turbines, Siemens SGT800 gas turbines, reciprocating engines, battery energy storage systems (BESS), substations, and associated balance of plant infrastructure. The scope includes major civil, mechanical, electrical, structural, underground utility, control systems, and commissioning works required to support phased energization and turnover of the facility. Your technical advice will help ensure defects, behaviors, and other issues are rectified prior to final inspection.

Provide specialized welding & inspection support for construction to ensure construction meets all code and Bechtel procedures including those found in the welding control manual.
Help manage the issue of weld filler materials for welders to meet Bechtel and Code requirements.
Help plan NDE activities of welding and coordinate with construction and Bechtel LFWE to support the overall construction schedule.
Demonstrate a personal commitment to safety.
Implementation of the project ES&H program and quality control program. 
Supporting the maintenance of calibrations as required for Pre/ PWHT equipment.
Working knowledge of our corporate Pre/PWHT Procedure ( PHT-2 ) and other welding procedures/standards.
Perform inspections and documentation of all welding, NDE, and Pre/ PWHT activities in progress on a continuing basis.
Prepares material requisitions and maintain inventory for all Pre/PWHT equipment and materials.
Coordinate work activities and training of direct hire Pre/ PWHT personnel.
Support the crafts in meeting quality requirements in order to maintain cost and schedule.
Coach lower-level engineers within the welding discipline to ensure employees’ and Bechtel's needs are accomplished to achieve project and Bechtel goals . 
Maintain weld inspection tracking system using Bechtel specific automation tools.
Supervise a team of Welding Field Engineers and clerks.

 Required bachelor's degree(or equivalent) and 8-10 years of relevant experience or 12-14 years of relevant work experience
Required Knowledge and Skills:
 
NDE Certs including PT, MT, and LT (Vac Box). Knowledge of AWS, API, NFPA, AWWA, ASTM and ASNT codes, specifications, and standards.
Must be certifiable by written examination to Bechtel's nondestructive examination requirements as NDE Level II Visual Examination.
Heavy industrial direct hire construction experience commensurate with the type of work to be undertaken.
Advanced knowledge and/or certifications in SMAW / GTAW / GMAW / FCAW.
Advanced knowledge of Piping Design Drawings / Specifications and TEAMWorks / WITS.
Intermediate understanding of Preheat/ Post weld heat treatment of equipment and consumables.
Knowledge of engineering and construction methods and processes customarily acquired over time through specialized instruction or practical experience.

Experience on power plants construction (preferred).

Project Overview:
 
Port Arthur LNG, LLC (PALNG) is developing a Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) production and export facility on a site located on the western shoreline of the Port Arthur Canal in Jefferson County, Texas. The project base scope will include two (2) identical LNG trains, two (2) LNG Storage Tanks, one (1) LNG Carrier Berth, and associated utilities/facilities. The Project is to be constructed on a parcel of land located along State Highway 87 south of Port Arthur, Texas, between the inner coastal waterway bridge and Keith Lake. The Infrastructure Global   Business Unit (GBU) is executing site civil and marine scopes of work associated with our scopes of work on the Energy GBU LNG projects.   As the #1 LNG EPC contractor by installed capacity, Bechtel has the capability to successfully deliver a broad range of LNG projects through our integrated EPC self-perform model, strategic partnerships, and proven experience in LNG project delivery. Your technical advice will help ensure defects, behaviors, and other issues are rectified prior to final inspection.

Implements and maintains Bechtel Field Welding program for all welding operations.  
Provides technical assistance to field superintendents and crafts.  
Ensures that all supervision and craft personnel involved in welding operations are educated and aware of applicable code and project requirements.

Required Knowledge and Skills:

NDE Certs including PT, MT and LT (Vac Box).

Specialization & Expertise:

Job Summary:
 
In this role, you will will support the installation of all underground piping and related Balance of Plant commodities for multiple General Electric 7HA Simple Cycle power generating systems and their auxiliary support systems. Duties include ensuring current and approved Installation Work Packages are readily available for the craft professionals. You will also be responsible for interpreting piping design drawings, specifications, piping installation/fabrication including pipe hangers and inline piping components. Provide field verification of materials and technical direction to field piping personnel as required for works related to the installation of multiple Simple Cycle power generation systems and Balance of Plant systems. #LI-RM1

Quantities

Technical  
Proactively considers continuous learning or shows interest to pursue appropriate learning activities 
System Turnover Coordinator

Develops firm understanding of system and area scoping and drawings required for package development.

Project Overview:
 
Bechtel is the licensing, engineering, procurement, and construction partner on TerraPower’s Natrium team. Selected by the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) for its Advanced Reactor Demonstration Program, Natrium is participating in a DOE initiative to re-establish U.S. leadership in nuclear energy that provides matching funding to industry to design and build an operational advanced reactor. TerraPower, a leading advanced generation technology company founded by former Microsoft CEO Bill Gates, will be deploying its 345 MWe sodium fast reactor (SFR), called Natrium. The Natrium plant features an advanced, cost-competitive reactor design that is safer, simpler, easier, and less costly to construct, less expensive to operate, and able to provide energy that is competitive with fossil fuels. It also features an innovative molten salt energy storage system designed to be complementary to solar and wind power by evening out the peaks and valleys in production when the sun is not shining or the wind is not blowing. With the reactor and energy storage output combined, the plant can produce more than 500 MWe of power. Other Team Natrium partners include GE Hitachi Nuclear Energy, Energy Northwest, PacifiCorp, and Duke Energy, along with support from a number of national laboratories and universities. This DOE Program has the potential to be the largest funding program for advanced nuclear power in U.S. history and will shape the future of nuclear energy for decades. We invite you to join our team for this historic and interesting opportunity! 
 
Job Summary:
 
In this role, you will lead, implement, and maintain global construction field welding programs and provide specialized welding engineering support. You will manage, inspect, and monitor the welding test lab, the rod room, the weld heat treatment program, and the laboratory quality plan. Your technical advice will help ensure defects, behaviors, and other issues are rectified prior to final inspection. 
#LI-BH1
Major Responsibilities:
 
Support inspection activities using ITP and engineering drawings.
Provide specialized welding & inspection support for construction to ensure construction meets all code and Bechtel procedures including those found in the welding control manual.
Help manage the issue of weld filler materials for welders to meet Bechtel and Code requirements.
Help plan NDE activities of welding and coordinate with construction and Bechtel LFWE to support the overall construction schedule.
Demonstrate a personal commitment to safety.
Implementation of the project ES&H program and quality control program. 
Supporting the maintenance of calibrations as required for Pre/ PWHT equipment.
Working knowledge of our corporate Pre/PWHT Procedure ( PHT-2 ) and other welding procedures/standards.
Perform inspections and documentation of all welding, NDE, and Pre/ PWHT activities in progress on a continuing basis.
Prepares material requisitions and maintain inventory for all Pre/PWHT equipment and materials.
Coordinate work activities and training of direct hire Pre/ PWHT personnel.
Support the crafts in meeting quality requirements in order to maintain cost and schedule.
Coach lower-level engineers within the welding discipline to ensure employees’ and Bechtel's needs are accomplished to achieve project and Bechtel goals . 
Maintain weld inspection tracking system using Bechtel specific automation tools. Interface with the Lead Welding Engineer for direction of work assigned tasks. 
Education and Experience Requirements:
 
Requires bachelor's degree (or international equivalent) and 8-10 years of relevant experience or 12-14 years of relevant work experience​
Required Knowledge and Skills:
 
NDE Certs including PT, MT, and LT (Vac Box). Knowledge of AWS, API, NFPA, AWWA, ASTM and ASNT codes, specifications, and standards.
Must be certifiable by written examination to Bechtel's nondestructive examination requirements as NDE Level II Visual Examination.
Heavy industrial direct hire construction experience commensurate with the type of work to be undertaken.
Advanced knowledge and/or certifications in SMAW / GTAW / GMAW / FCAW.
Advanced knowledge of Piping Design Drawings / Specifications and TEAMWorks / WITS.
Intermediate understanding of Preheat/ Post weld heat treatment of equipment and consumables.
Knowledge of engineering and construction methods and processes customarily acquired over time through specialized instruction or practical experience.
Demonstrated ability to motivate team members effectively & positively in a collaborative team environment. 
Demonstrated ability to problem-solve in high pressure situations. Strong technical background and knowledge of all phases of LNG welding applications and methods, and non-destructive examination relating to LNG construction activities.
Experience on an LNG construction site along the U.S. Gulf Coast.
Understanding of applicable Bechtel work processes including Standard Work Process Procedures.
Knowledge and understanding of Bechtel's SWPP 4MP-T81-03701 Welding Program, 03708 Pre/ Post Weld Heat Treatment and 03705 Welding & NDE Documentation.
Familiarity with Bechtel Standard Applications (BSAPs) including Unifier, Aconex, DSCS, PTR, Agile, Navisworks, WITs, TEAMWorks, Arc Stryker, PipeFighter, and other automation tools which are used on Bechtel projects.  
 
Additional Information: Work requires the physical ability to conduct field inspections including but not limited to: extensive walking in and around a construction site often in high heat and high humidity conditions, climbing ladders, climbing scaffolding, working on elevated platforms, working on uneven surfaces, and working in small or confined spaces. Some lifting may be required. Must have the ability to walk into and out of the project (enter/egress). Must be able to use appropriate standard issued Personal Protection Equipment (PPE) required for each assigned task (i.e. harness, life vest, lift buckets, etc.).

Project Overview:
 
Bechtel is working for Northrop Grumman Corp. to deliver the Engineering, Manufacturing, and Development (EMD) portion of the Sentinel Program.  A system of inter-continental ballistic missiles and launch infrastructure. Sentinel is a vital U.S. Air Force program designed to modernize the nation’s aging force of inter-continental ballistic missiles, which make up the ground element of the nuclear deterrent. As part of Northrop Grumman’s Sentinel team, Bechtel is providing launch infrastructure design, construction, and integration. A major contractor for the United States and allied governments, Bechtel has handled such efforts as chemical weapons demilitarization projects, missile-defense, infrastructure, base operations, procurement and project management, and restoration and recovery spanning half a century. We use our expertise to help our customers securely and effectively transform mission delivery.  We invite you to join our team for this very interesting opportunity! 
Job Summary:
 
The Project Engineer, reporting to the Project Engineering Manager, oversees the design development of project facilities, ensuring all system requirements are met through customer review and approval. This role supervises multidisciplinary engineering activities, interprets and coordinates assignments, and independently resolves technical issues. Responsibilities include aligning site-specific engineering documents, coordinating remote teams and resources, developing procedures, and managing staffing. The PE ensures consistent use of standard planning tools, resolves quality issues, leads support teams, and manages planning, scheduling, deliverables, and performance metrics for the Sentinel project.   
#LI-TN1
Major Responsibilities:
 
Directs and coordinates engineering activities for the assigned portion of the project.
Establishes or assists in establishing the detailed project execution plan and the development of the overall project engineering budget and schedule.
Inputs to and owns the engineering elements of a project capital estimate (proposals and execution) quantities and budgets.
Performs and manages the work in accordance with relevant Engineering Department Procedure Instructions (EDPIs) issued on the project, design guides and Bechtel Standard Application Programs (BSAPs).
Develops design and procurement schedules for approval and establishes engineering budget and schedule for portions of a project or task.
Prepares project procedures for engineering, as required.
Coordinates engineering effort with Procurement, Construction, Operations, Startup, Environmental Safety and Health (ES&H), Client Regulators and Project Controls.
Reviews and approves design documents, change orders, material requisitions and specifications for Construction or Procurement use.
Supervises the preparation of, reviews and endorses bid analyses and recommendations for procurement of equipment material and services within the Engineering scope.
Coordinates activities among the design groups on the project.
Initiates requests for specialized professional services as well as coordinate with other functions (procurement, construction, startup, project controls, and estimating etc.) to ensure alignment of data and deliverables.
Reviews project engineering progress against approved schedules, evaluates changes and takes corrective action when required.
Prepares or directs the preparation of project studies, reports, forecasts, special engineering reports and plant data book.
Communicates complex technical issues and recommends solutions to PEM and PM
Education and Experience Requirements:
 
Requires bachelor's degree (or international equivalent) and 10-13  years of relevant experience or 14-17 years of relevant work experience​
Required Knowledge and Skills:
 
Have an active Professional Engineering license
Must be able to pass a drug/background check.
Already acquired or able to obtain DOD Top Secret Clearance which requires a US citizenship. 
 
Minimum Qualifications: 
Technical knowledge of related engineering systems, engineering calculations, applications of engineering and construction methods and materials, and engineering application of computers. 
Proficient knowledge of Bechtel engineering processes and procedures. 
Experience working with government agencies, preferably Department of Defense (DoD). 
Experience with or knowledge of non-engineering functions such as Project Controls, Construction, Startup, Procurement, and Estimating. 
Experience with or knowledge of interfacing with other engineering disciplines to deliver standard NS&E engineering deliverables and understanding the key handoffs for the products delivered. 
Experience with or knowledge of procurement of components from vendors on a government project and interfacing with both vendors and procurements to successfully deliver. 
Experienced in configuration control and requirements management using DOORS
At least 2 years of relevant experience as a Cost Account Manager (CAM) at a large DOE or DOD project.  
Preferred Qualifications: 
Experience with engineering planning and tracking tools including SmartStaff, Unifier, and advanced excel skills. 
Experience developing equipment configuration packages for procurement and maintenance. 
Experience in the design of complex industrial facilities. 
Experience developing equipment configuration packages for procurement and maintenance. 
Experience with Primavera P6 schedule and reviewing schedule and providing status to Project Controls.

Job Summary:
 
LOTO Tagging Authority (TA) will report to the Lead LOTO Tagging Authority and is responsible for receiving and acting on requests to perform work on components or systems that are energized or have the potential to be energized. The TA will be responsible for energy isolation management in accordance with corporate and project specific procedures. Must be willing to work a flexible schedule if required.
Acting as the primary on-site LOTO Tagging Authority for commissioning, this position works closely with engineering, construction, operations, vendors, equipment suppliers, commissioning teams, and Authorities Having Jurisdiction (AHJs) to develop and execute comprehensive testing and validation programs. The role provides technical leadership throughout system turnover, integrated systems testing, regulatory inspections, and operational readiness activities to ensure all life safety systems are fully functional, code-compliant, documented, and ready for safe occupancy and operation.
•    Collaborate: Actively seeks out others’ opinions and input and is open-minded to their views. Challenges others appropriately and is also open to being challenged on their own views. 
•    Communicate: Is effective at adapting their communication style appropriately to their audience, providing context and using the right tone and level of information. 
•    Customer Focus: Prioritizes the customer needs and puts these at the forefront of all interactions. Ensures that others within their team maintain this same approach.
•    Deliver: Demonstrates making well thought out, timely decisions and prioritization that leads to successful outcomes. Includes the right people at the right time. 
•    Problem Solve: Accurately assesses a situation to find the best solution or way of working in order to resolve the issues. Is focused on creating a long term fix rather than a short-term solution.
•    Role Model: Contributes to the Bechtel culture by inspiring and motivating others through their positive approach and attitude. Comes to work determined to do a good job, for themselves and for their team. .

Major Responsibilities:
 
Manage the integrated LOTO program on the Project coordinating between subcontractors, JV partners, and all other affected entities to deliver a safe and uniform program for the control of work and isolation of hazardous sources of energy.
Ensure the energy isolation program complies with SWPP's, STCP's, and Q4Safety. Use Q4Safety for electronic Energy Isolation Permits (EIP's).
Provide input and support to the Construction, Operations, and Commissioning teams, in piping, mechanical, and electrical isolations.
Identify isolation boundaries based on work scope and develop and issue EIP's.
Administer and manage the energy isolation program with strict enforcement and record keeping.
Perform other duties as directed in support of project activities.
Coordinate activities with other disciplines or areas and client representatives.
Maintain the training program and register on the project related to EIP's.
Procure material required to support the energy isolation management program.
Monitors the activities of subcontractors to ensure compliance with energy isolation.
Problem resolution and inspection of EIP's.
Monitor the installation of LOTO equipment such as Locks and Tags etc.
Work hours as required, including overtime, weekends, and holidays, as necessary to support the project.
Education and Experience Requirements:
 
Requires bachelor's degree (or international equivalent) and 8-10  years of relevant experience or 12-14 years of relevant work experience
Required Knowledge and Skills:
 
Construction experience assigned to a construction site.
Heavy industrial direct hire construction experience.
Experience in oil, gas, refinery, petrochemical, minerals processing, or power generating station construction.
Knowledge of engineering and construction methods and processes customarily acquired over time through specialized instruction or practical experience.
Demonstrated ability to motivate and lead team members effectively & positively in a collaborative team environment.
Demonstrated ability to problem-solve in high pressure situations.
Computer literate in word processing and spreadsheet and database applications.
Preferred: Experience on an LNG construction site especially along the U.S. Gulf Coast.
Preferred: Understanding of applicable Bechtel processes including Standard Work Process Procedures.
Preferred: Advanced knowledge of Project Bechtel Standard Applications (BSAPs) including Setroute, Unifier, Aconex, DSCS, PTR, Agile, Navisworks, and other automation tools which are used on Bechtel projects.
Experience managing technical services subcontracts.
Additional Information: 
Work requires the physical ability to conduct field inspections including but not limited to: extensive walking in and around a construction site often in high heat and high humidity conditions, climbing ladders, climbing scaffolding, working on elevated platforms, working on uneven surfaces, and working in small or confined spaces. 
Some lifting may be required. Must have the ability to walk into and out of the project (enter/egress). 
Must be able to use appropriate standard issued Personal Protection Equipment (PPE) required for each assigned task (i.e. harness, life vest, lift buckets, etc.).
